title: Mycelial Computing
intent: Distributed computation inspired by fungal mycelium networks, where information processing emerges through chemical signaling, network topology, and adaptive self-organization.

noise_to_insight.⇑: Chemical slowness reframed as temporal filtering — noise rejected through delay
noise_to_insight.ᘯᘰ: Network fragmentation reframed as pruning — damage reveals optimal pathways
noise_to_insight.⚙: Bio-digital interface gap reframed as translation layer and design grammar
noise_to_insight.◎: Incomplete integration reframed as productive boundary between bio and digital layers

insight: A living computer grown from fungal logic, where slowness is filtering, fragmentation is pruning, and the gap between biology and silicon becomes a generative boundary. The mycelium does not process information — it becomes information, threading chemical memory through soil, rewriting its own topology with every cycle of growth and decay.
